negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Async pool metadata insertion does not derive commit_block as expiry_block - ttl and does not compute two-phase deadlines from such a derived commit block.
related_but_insufficient_code: The port process_async_commitment_transactions receives commit_block from the canonical tip and computes contract_expiry_block as commit_block + async_commitment.ttl.
AsyncTxMetadata stores commit_block supplied by canonical processing and computes only phase2 offset from precompile input; it does not derive commit_block from expiry_block - ttl.

notes: Confirmed gap; the current metadata flow derives expiry from canonical commit_block, the reverse of the invariant.
rationale: The async metadata candidate states a concrete current formula for deriving commit block and phase-two deadlines from expiry and ttl rather than trusting a possibly divergent commitment field.
statement: At current tip, AsyncCommitmentPool metadata insertion must derive commit_block as expiry_block - ttl and must compute two-phase deadline offsets from that derived commit_block rather than from async_commitment.commit_block.
suggested_falsification_tests: Insert an async commitment with expiry_block 120, ttl 20, phase-two offset 7, and an intentionally different async_commitment.commit_block; assert metadata stores commit_block 100 and phase-two deadline 107.
summary: Async pool commit-block-from-expiry derivation is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Insert commitment with expiry_block=120, ttl=20, and divergent commitment commit_block; current port has no expiry_block input and stores canonical commit_block instead.
title: Async pool derives commit block from expiry
